MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-16-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Rosie Badgett of GORDON & REES LLP (619-544-7223), appearing for defendant and moving party The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Wilson Parker and Patricia Ann Parker.

RULING: DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUCATION
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kimberly L. Avery of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5074 (Tel: 510-451-1580). (503/CML)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancng The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and moving parties William Parker and Patricia Parker
Laura Przetak of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), designated defense counsel
Anna C. Mourlam of GORDON & REES LLP (510-463-8656), appearing for defendant and opposing party Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company
Alice Wong of McDERMOTT WILL & EMERY (650) 815-7400, appearing for defendant and opposing party Honeywell International Inc.
Jerel P. Agatep of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant Douglas Insulation Company, Inc.
Edward P. Tugade of DEMLER, ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP (415-757-3493), appearing for defendant and joinder to Opposition Pneumo Abex LLC

RULING:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for order advancing the current trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The November 5, 2018 trial date is advanced to August 20, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is August 30, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 16, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
(Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancng The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

Fact discovery cut-off date is August 3, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 17, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 1, 2018. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than May 16, 2018 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than May 16, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on June 27, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than July 6, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than July 20, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by August 13, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, August 16, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. The responses filed by Honeywell International Inc. and Pneumo Abex LLC were not considered for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, not reported. (503/LOT)

Off Calendar Oct-31-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Wilson Parker and Patricia
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Megan F. Clark of GORDON & REES LLP (510-463-8689), appearing for defendant / opposing party The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company
James V. Weixel of DEMLER, ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP (415-949-1900), appearing via CourtCall, for defendant Pneumo Abex LLC, joinder to Goodyear's opposition
Daniel D. O'Shea of PERKINS COIE (415-344-7002), appearing via CourtCall, for defendant Honeywell International Inc., joinder to Goodyear's opposition
Janice W. Man of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant Douglas Insulation Company, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial, and extending discovery cutoff is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate that Mr. Parker's health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation, as required by C.C.P. 36(a). The joinder to opposition filed by Honeywell International Inc. was not considered for failure to lodge a courtesy copy with the Court. (S.F. Local Rules 2.7(B), 2.11(T).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Pursuant to plaintiffs' request the Court continued plaintiffs' motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial, and extending discovery cutoff to Tuesday, October 31, 2017 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. The Court allowed plaintiffs and joinder Honeywell International Inc. to submit their courtesy copies to the courtroom before the end of today. The Court further determined that if the motion granted at the next hearing, last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) would be February 28, 2018. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)
